DECLARATION OF LILY A. NORTH IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL CONFIDENTIAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS - ***REDACTED*** (TRANSACTION ID # 65756056) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL CONFIDENTIAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS.GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Attorney, Ruby H. Kazi (email: RKazi@beneschlaw.com) of the law offices of BENESCH, FRIEDLANDER, COPLAN & ARONOFF LLP (Tel: 628.600.2250) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Matthew J. Gluck (email:cmgluck@gluckdaniel.com) of the law offices of GLUCK DANIEL LLP (Tel: 415.510.2604) specially appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Cause on calendar: PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with words of advice for counsel as set forth in the notes of the court reporter.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff Doordash, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted. Defendants 901 Market St SF LLC a/k/a Knotel LLC and Knotel, Inc. shall serve verified, code-compliant responses to Form Interrogatories 15.1 and 17.1 within two weeks of notice of this order. All objections (except attorney-client privilege and attorney work product) are overruled.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Set For Trial On May-03-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiff Doordash, Inc.?S Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Portions Of The Complaint And Exhibits
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.'s Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Portions Of The Complaint And Exhibits.

Granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
